title: Port Scan from Security Scanner
description: Nessus scanner (10.0.0.5) performing scheduled TCP scan against 10.20.0.0/24. Scan window 09:00-11:00 UTC per change record CHG-20260321-011. No anomalous targets.

title: User Account Lockout
description: Account 'p.vasquez' locked out on Outlook Web Access after 5 failed password attempts from 10.30.5.14. User submitted helpdesk ticket TKT-88500 immediately after. Likely forgotten password.

title: USB Device Connected
description: USB mass storage device connected to LAPTOP-NB12 by user n.brown. Device: SanDisk Cruzer 16GB. No auto-run events. Device policy allows personal storage for this user group.

title: Windows Defender Quarantine
description: Defender quarantined PUA.Win32.CoinMiner.gen on DESKTOP-CR03. File: C:\Users\c.reed\Downloads\game_patch_v2.exe. Detected during on-demand scan. No active mining observed.

title: Port Scan from Security Scanner
description: Qualys WAS scan (10.0.0.6) against web application tier 10.90.0.0/24. Authenticated scan with service account qualys_scan. Consistent with weekly AppSec baseline.

title: User Account Lockout
description: Account 'w.kim' locked after 5 consecutive failed logins on VPN gateway (10.0.0.1). Source IP 198.51.100.22 is a known residential ISP range. User traveling per HR records.

title: USB Device Connected
description: USB audio device connected to WORKSTATION-EJ05 by e.jackson. Device: Jabra Evolve 65 headset. Audio HID class. No removable storage. Standard peripheral.

title: Windows Defender Quarantine
description: Defender quarantined Adware.Win32.BHO.gen on LAPTOP-YC01. File: C:\Users\y.carter\AppData\Roaming\BrowserExt\ext.dll. Browser extension hijacker, no lateral movement observed.

title: Port Scan from Security Scanner
description: OpenVAS scan (10.0.0.5) against 10.10.0.0/24 internal subnet. Daily compliance run per policy POL-NET-003. No new findings flagged relative to prior baseline.

title: User Account Lockout
description: Account 'b.osei' locked on Active Directory from DESKTOP-BO08 (10.40.2.19). 5 failed attempts over 10 minutes. User confirmed via desk visit — keyboard sticky keys causing mistype.

title: DNS Query to Known C2 Domain
description: Host WORKSTATION-EJ05 (10.80.6.22) issued DNS query for cdn-update.fastserv[.]cc, flagged in TI feed as active C2 infrastructure. Query resolved to 91.108.4.177. Possible beaconing.

title: Suspicious Outbound Beacon
description: Regular HTTPS POST requests from WORKSTATION-EJ05 (10.80.6.22) to 91.108.4.177 (cdn-update.fastserv[.]cc) every 60 seconds. Payload size 512 bytes, consistent with C2 heartbeat/beacon pattern.

title: Data Staging to Temp Directory
description: Large volume of file copy activity on WORKSTATION-EJ05: 2.3 GB staged to C:\Windows\Temp\cab\ over 4 minutes via robocopy.exe. Source paths include Documents, Desktop, mapped drives. Consistent with pre-exfiltration staging.

title: Ransomware Behavior Detected
description: FILESERVER02 (10.10.3.20): svc.exe (PID 2204) initiating mass file rename operations — appending '.locked' extension to files across 14 network shares. 3,800 files renamed in 90 seconds. Encryption in progress.

title: Mass File Encryption in Progress
description: FILESERVER02 (10.10.3.20): Shadow copy deletion detected (vssadmin delete shadows /all /quiet). 12,400 files encrypted across \\FILESERVER02\finance$, \\FILESERVER02\hr$, \\FILESERVER02\legal$. HOW_TO_RESTORE.txt dropped in each directory. Immediate isolation required.
